pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_task.sh which copies the example rocket file to this location.
Confirmed via explicit task description instructions to save the file here.
Confirmed via explicit task description instructions to write the report here.
Task description requires an outer diameter of 66 mm, equating to a radius of 33 mm (0.033 m).
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value for radius tolerance checks.
Task description defines the existing outer diameter as ~24.8 mm, equating to a radius of 12.4 mm (0.0124 m).
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value for the original radius tolerance checks.
Task description requires adding a Mass Component of 80 g (0.08 kg).
Confirmed via verifier.py which sets this mass tolerance limit.
Task description explicitly requires a Body Tube length of 'at least 100 mm' (0.1 m), contradicting the lenient 0.095 m claim in metadata.
Task description requires the flight simulation to reach an apogee of at least 50 meters.

privileged_info_summary: The input data is a real sample '.ork' file from OpenRocket. The task description explicitly mandates a payload body tube length of at least 100 mm (0.1 m), contradicting the metadata's claim of 0.095 m. All other metadata parameters accurately match the task instructions and verifier scripts, including a 0.033 m target radius (66 mm diameter), a 0.0124 m original radius (24.8 mm diameter), a 0.08 kg CanSat mass, and a 50.0 m minimum apogee.
